content: Just as neurobiological research supports a unified memory framework that integrates individual experienced episodes and general abstract semantic knowledge via the Mental Time Travel (MTT) system, biological and chemical agents can be modeled as unified episodic-semantic graphs to capture both their unique interaction histories with specific micro-environments and their general functional causal properties. This hybrid graph structure for agents would enable triple hybrid retrieval that combines geometric embedding matching and context-aware traversal to predict an agent's future effects in novel environments more accurately than separating historical and general knowledge.
